name: Routing Log (Postgres)
description: Per-turn routing decisions: the field-level sensitivity tag applied at Gate 1, the capability decision at Gate 2, and the outcome for every field and task. The source of truth for the Field Sovereignty Ledger — it is what proves member data resolved in-environment and that zero PII reached the frontier model.

name: LLM Usage Log
description: Which tasks ran on the in-environment SLM and which escalated to the frontier model, with the justification for each escalation. Behind the frontier task share, the 88/12 token split, and the monthly usage view.

name: KAG Knowledge Graph (Neo4j)
description: The governance graph: field nodes, source-system nodes, and policy nodes (rules like DG-04 and DG-07) with their relationships. Decides what is sensitive and where it originates — classification by meaning, not keyword matching — and feeds the Gate 1 sovereignty decision.

name: LiteLLM Gateway
description: The unified model gateway in front of the in-environment SLM and the frontier model (Claude Sonnet on Azure). Emits token counts, the SLM/frontier split, and per-task cost as each request is routed.

name: LiteLLM Cost Records (Postgres)
description: Per-task and per-session cost with the SLM versus frontier split, plus the modelled all-frontier counterfactual used to measure what routing saves. Governs cost across every persona on the platform.

name: Agent Observability Log
description: Per-turn record of every agent action: the query, the response, the context it used, its chain of thought, and the policy or business rule that governed it. Powers the single-pane observability view.

name: Grafana (via API)
description: Agent health and platform telemetry behind the observability dashboard.

name: Model Registry
description: The two models in production: the in-environment SLM (Azure) and the frontier model, Claude Sonnet (Azure), with versions, deployment environment, and routing eligibility.
